text: Normally this wouldn’t be an issue , as tropical cyclones tend to track from east to west , so a storm that forms west of 90W is unlikely to cross east of 90W . But Milton is a highly unusual exception , and ECMWF’s ensemble tracker apparently doesn’t handle crossing basins from west to east .
author: burgwx.bsky.social

text: There is a long technical explanation for why ECMWF’s tracker is having issues , but the short version is it partitions tropical cyclones into basins , and their Atlantic & Pacific basin border is delineated around 90W . The problem ? Milton is west of 90W and crossing that boundary from west to east .
